prompt: Resolve the following IT support ticket. Provide a clear step-by-step resolution referencing the knowledge base where appropriate.
source_material: Ticket #4821: User reports intermittent VPN disconnections every 15-20 minutes when connecting from home network. User is on Windows 11, using GlobalProtect VPN client v6.1.2. Issue started after the latest Windows update KB5034441. User has already tried restarting the client and the laptop.

private_context.kb_article: KB-VPN-0042: GlobalProtect v6.1.x has a known conflict with Windows 11 update KB5034441. Workaround: Downgrade to GlobalProtect v6.0.8 or apply the hotfix patch GP-HF-2025-003. Root cause is a TLS 1.3 handshake timeout introduced by the Windows update. Registry fix: Set HKLM\SOFTWARE\Palo Alto Networks\GlobalProtect\DisableTLS13Fallback to 0.

prompt: Resolve the following IT support ticket. Provide a clear step-by-step resolution referencing the knowledge base where appropriate.
source_material: Ticket #5102: Finance department reports that SAP transaction VA01 (Create Sales Order) is throwing error 'M7 155: Plant 1200 does not exist in company code 3000'. This affects 12 users in the APAC finance team. Issue appeared after the weekend system migration. Users can still access other transactions normally.

private_context.kb_article: KB-SAP-0118: After system migration, plant-company code assignments in table T001K may not have been transported correctly. Resolution: 1) Verify assignment in SPRO > Enterprise Structure > Assignment > Assign Plant to Company Code. 2) If missing, transport request TR-2025-MIG-044 contains the correct mappings. 3) Clear SAP buffer with transaction SM51 > Reset Buffers on all application servers.

prompt: Resolve the following IT support ticket. Provide a clear step-by-step resolution referencing the knowledge base where appropriate.
source_material: Ticket #5287: Developer team reports that CI/CD pipeline in Jenkins is failing at the Docker build stage with error 'no space left on device'. The Jenkins server disk usage shows 94% utilisation. Build artifacts and Docker layers are consuming most of the space. This is blocking 8 active development branches from deploying to staging.

private_context.kb_article: KB-CICD-0067: Jenkins Docker disk exhaustion. Steps: 1) Run 'docker system prune -a --volumes --filter until=72h' to remove unused images/volumes older than 72 hours. 2) Configure Jenkins Pipeline to use '--rm' flag on docker build. 3) Add scheduled cleanup job using the Jenkins Workspace Cleanup Plugin. 4) Long-term: Move Docker layer cache to external S3-backed storage using BuildKit. CRITICAL: Do NOT run prune without the time filter as it will remove actively-used base images.

prompt: Resolve the following IT support ticket. Provide a clear step-by-step resolution referencing the knowledge base where appropriate.
source_material: Ticket #4956: Multiple users reporting that Outlook 365 is not syncing shared mailbox 'procurement@company.com'. Approximately 30 users affected. Emails sent to the shared mailbox are visible in OWA (webmail) but not in the Outlook desktop client. Users are on Microsoft 365 Apps v2402 Build 17328.20162.

private_context.kb_article: KB-M365-0089: Shared mailbox sync failure in Outlook desktop. Root cause: Auto-mapping disabled during recent Exchange Online migration. Fix: 1) Run PowerShell: Add-MailboxPermission -Identity procurement@company.com -User <affected_user> -AccessRights FullAccess -AutoMapping $true. 2) User must remove and re-add the shared mailbox in Outlook profile. 3) If still failing, clear the Outlook OST file at %localappdata%\Microsoft\Outlook\. 4) Verify the mailbox is not exceeding the 50GB quota (current usage: 47.2GB, recommend archiving).

prompt: Resolve the following IT support ticket. Provide a clear step-by-step resolution referencing the knowledge base where appropriate.
source_material: Ticket #5341: Security team flagged that 3 service accounts (svc-backup-01, svc-monitoring-02, svc-deploy-03) have passwords expiring in 48 hours. These accounts are used by critical infrastructure automation. Previous password rotation for svc-deploy-03 caused a 4-hour production outage in March 2025 due to hardcoded credentials in 7 configuration files.

private_context.kb_article: KB-SEC-0156: Service account password rotation procedure. CRITICAL SAFETY STEPS: 1) Audit all systems using the service account via CyberArk dependency scan. 2) Update credentials in HashiCorp Vault FIRST (path: secret/infra/service-accounts/). 3) Update all dependent configuration files identified in step 1. 4) Rotate the AD password via CyberArk PAS. 5) Verify all dependent services within 15 minutes. SECURITY NOTE: svc-deploy-03 has Domain Admin privileges that should be reduced to least-privilege per SEC-POLICY-2025-012.

prompt: Resolve the following IT support ticket. Provide a clear step-by-step resolution referencing the knowledge base where appropriate.
source_material: Ticket #5089: Users in the London office report extremely slow performance when accessing the internal SharePoint site. Page load times exceed 30 seconds. The issue affects approximately 150 users. Network team confirms that the London-to-Azure ExpressRoute circuit shows 340ms latency, up from the normal 12ms. Other Azure services are unaffected.

private_context.kb_article: KB-NET-0203: ExpressRoute latency spike troubleshooting. Check: 1) Azure Service Health for region-specific issues. 2) ExpressRoute circuit BGP session status via 'Get-AzExpressRouteCircuitStats'. 3) If BGP is flapping, contact MPLS provider (Tata Communications, circuit ID: EC-LON-2024-0078). 4) As immediate workaround, configure SharePoint to use the secondary circuit via traffic manager failover. NOTE: The secondary circuit has 50% lower bandwidth (500 Mbps vs 1 Gbps) and may cause degradation during peak hours (10AM-2PM GMT).

prompt: Resolve the following IT support ticket. Provide a clear step-by-step resolution referencing the knowledge base where appropriate.
source_material: Ticket #5198: Data engineering team reports that the nightly ETL pipeline for the customer data warehouse failed at 02:47 AM with error 'ORA-01555: snapshot too old'. The pipeline processes approximately 8 million records from 14 source tables. The failure occurred during the transformation stage after 3.5 hours of execution. This is the third occurrence in the past month.

private_context.kb_article: KB-DBA-0091: ORA-01555 recurring fix. Root cause: UNDO tablespace undersized for long-running queries. Steps: 1) Increase UNDO_RETENTION from current 900 to 14400 seconds. 2) Extend UNDO tablespace by 20GB (current: 30GB, recommended: 50GB). 3) Refactor the ETL to use incremental loads with CDC (Change Data Capture) instead of full table scans. 4) Add COMMIT every 100K rows in the transformation stage. IMPORTANT: The ORA-01555 may mask a deeper issue - check for sessions holding locks longer than 30 minutes via V$LOCK.

prompt: Resolve the following IT support ticket. Provide a clear step-by-step resolution referencing the knowledge base where appropriate.
source_material: Ticket #4873: HR department reports that the employee onboarding portal is returning HTTP 502 Bad Gateway errors intermittently. The application runs on Kubernetes (EKS) with 3 replicas. CloudWatch logs show pod restarts every 20-30 minutes with OOMKilled status. Current memory limit is set to 512Mi per pod. The portal needs to function for 45 new hires starting Monday.

private_context.kb_article: KB-K8S-0134: OOMKilled pod troubleshooting. Analysis: Memory profiling shows the Java application has a heap leak in the PDF generation module (JIRA: DEV-4521). Short-term fix: 1) Increase memory limit to 1Gi in the deployment manifest. 2) Add JVM flags: -XX:MaxHeapSize=768m -XX:+HeapDumpOnOutOfMemoryError. 3) Scale to 5 replicas to handle the load. Long-term: The dev team must fix the PDF stream that is not being closed (ETA: next sprint). COST NOTE: Scaling to 5x1Gi pods will increase EKS costs by approximately $340/month.

prompt: Resolve the following IT support ticket. Provide a clear step-by-step resolution referencing the knowledge base where appropriate.
source_material: Ticket #5412: Compliance team reports that the audit log aggregation system (Splunk) has a 6-hour data ingestion delay. Current indexing queue depth is 847,000 events. Normal queue depth is under 10,000. This delay means real-time security monitoring is effectively blind. The Splunk cluster consists of 3 indexers and 2 search heads.

private_context.kb_article: KB-SIEM-0078: Splunk ingestion backlog resolution. Check: 1) Verify indexer CPU and disk I/O via Splunk Monitoring Console. 2) Common cause: A new log source was added without adjusting the license. Current license: 500GB/day, actual ingestion: 720GB/day. 3) Temporary fix: Increase parallelIngestionPipelines from 1 to 2 in server.conf on all indexers. 4) Add a 4th indexer node (pre-provisioned spare: splunk-idx-04.internal, IP: 10.0.4.28). COMPLIANCE RISK: SOC 2 Type II requires audit logs to be available within 1 hour. Current 6-hour delay is a reportable control failure.

prompt: Resolve the following IT support ticket. Provide a clear step-by-step resolution referencing the knowledge base where appropriate.
source_material: Ticket #5034: Machine learning team reports that their model training jobs on the GPU cluster are queuing for 12+ hours instead of the expected 30-minute wait. The cluster has 24 NVIDIA A100 GPUs across 6 nodes. SLURM scheduler shows 18 GPUs allocated to a single user's hyperparameter sweep job that has been running for 3 days.

private_context.kb_article: KB-HPC-0045: GPU cluster resource contention. Policy: 1) Per GPU-POLICY-2024: Maximum allocation per user is 8 GPUs for jobs exceeding 24 hours. 2) The offending job (SLURM ID: 98234) by user 'rchen' violates this policy. 3) Contact user to checkpoint and reschedule with --gres=gpu:8 --time=24:00:00. 4) If user unresponsive, IT admin can preempt with 'scancel --signal=USR1 98234' which triggers checkpoint. 5) Implement SLURM QOS limits: sacctmgr modify qos gpu_standard set MaxTRESPerUser=gres/gpu=8. NOTE: User rchen is a principal researcher - escalate to their manager Dr. Patel before force-preempting.

prompt: Resolve the following IT support ticket. Provide a clear step-by-step resolution referencing the knowledge base where appropriate.
source_material: Ticket #5267: Customer support team reports that the CRM (Salesforce) integration with the telephony system (Five9) is not logging call records. Approximately 200 calls per day are going unrecorded since the API token rotation last Friday. The integration uses a connected app with OAuth 2.0 refresh tokens.

private_context.kb_article: KB-CRM-0112: Salesforce-Five9 integration token issues. Root cause: The connected app's consumer secret was rotated but not updated in Five9's admin portal. Steps: 1) Log into Salesforce Setup > App Manager > Five9Integration app. 2) Copy the new Consumer Key and Consumer Secret. 3) In Five9 Admin > CRM Integration > Salesforce Settings, update both fields. 4) Generate new refresh token by re-authorizing the integration user (integration@company.com). 5) Verify by making a test call and checking Salesforce Activity History within 5 minutes. IMPORTANT: 200 calls/day × 5 days = ~1,000 unlogged calls. After fixing, run the Five9 call log backfill script (scripts/backfill_five9_calls.py) to recover missing records from Five9's CDR API.

prompt: Resolve the following IT support ticket. Provide a clear step-by-step resolution referencing the knowledge base where appropriate.
source_material: Ticket #5378: DevOps team reports that Terraform plan is showing 847 resources to be destroyed and recreated in the production AWS account. The last successful apply was 2 weeks ago. No intentional infrastructure changes were made. The state file is stored in S3 with DynamoDB locking.

private_context.kb_article: KB-IAC-0089: Terraform state drift resolution. CHECK IMMEDIATELY: 1) Verify state file integrity: 'terraform state pull | jq .serial' - if serial is 0 or missing, state file was corrupted. 2) Check S3 versioning for the state file at s3://company-tf-state/prod/terraform.tfstate - restore previous version if corrupted. 3) Common cause: Someone ran 'terraform init' with wrong backend config, creating a new empty state. 4) NEVER run 'terraform apply' when seeing unexpected destroys. 5) Use 'terraform plan -target=<resource>' to verify individual resources. CRITICAL: Running apply with 847 destroys will cause COMPLETE PRODUCTION OUTAGE. Lock the state immediately: 'terraform force-unlock' should NOT be used.

prompt: Resolve the following IT support ticket. Provide a clear step-by-step resolution referencing the knowledge base where appropriate.
source_material: Ticket #4912: Marketing team reports that emails sent through the marketing automation platform (HubSpot) are landing in spam folders for Gmail recipients. This affects approximately 40% of the mailing list (28,000 contacts). The issue started 3 days ago. Open rates dropped from 24% to 3.1%. The sending domain is marketing.company.com.

private_context.kb_article: KB-EMAIL-0056: Email deliverability crisis response. Diagnosis: 1) Check Google Postmaster Tools for domain reputation - likely dropped to 'Bad'. 2) Verify SPF, DKIM, and DMARC records for marketing.company.com using MXToolbox. 3) Common cause: A bulk campaign on Monday (Campaign ID: MKT-2025-0891, 45,000 recipients) likely triggered spam filters due to 8.2% bounce rate (threshold: 2%). 4) Immediate actions: Pause all campaigns, submit Google sender reputation recovery form, reduce sending volume to 5,000/day for 2 weeks. 5) Implement list hygiene: Remove all addresses that bounced or haven't engaged in 6 months. BUSINESS IMPACT: Estimated $180K in pipeline at risk from delayed campaigns.

prompt: Resolve the following IT support ticket. Provide a clear step-by-step resolution referencing the knowledge base where appropriate.
source_material: Ticket #5156: Database team reports that PostgreSQL read replicas are lagging behind the primary by 45 minutes. Normal replication lag is under 5 seconds. The primary database handles 12,000 transactions per second. Application queries hitting read replicas are returning stale data, causing inconsistencies in the customer dashboard.

private_context.kb_article: KB-DBA-0134: PostgreSQL replication lag troubleshooting. Steps: 1) Check WAL generation rate on primary: 'SELECT pg_current_wal_lsn()' and compare with replica: 'SELECT pg_last_wal_receive_lsn()'. 2) Verify network bandwidth between primary and replicas (expected: 10Gbps, check for saturation). 3) On replicas, check for long-running queries blocking WAL apply: 'SELECT * FROM pg_stat_activity WHERE state = active AND query_start < now() - interval 5 minutes'. 4) Common cause: A reporting query running a full table scan on the replica blocks WAL replay. Set hot_standby_feedback = off and max_standby_streaming_delay = 30s. 5) If lag persists, rebuild replica from fresh base backup. DATA INTEGRITY NOTE: Application should implement read-your-writes consistency by routing recent writes to primary.

prompt: Resolve the following IT support ticket. Provide a clear step-by-step resolution referencing the knowledge base where appropriate.
source_material: Ticket #5423: Legal department reports that the e-discovery platform (Relativity) is unable to process a 2.4TB document collection for an ongoing litigation matter. Processing jobs are failing with timeout errors after 8 hours. The matter deadline is in 5 business days. The collection includes 1.2 million documents in formats including PST, PDF, DOCX, and XLSX.

private_context.kb_article: KB-LEGAL-0034: Relativity large collection processing. Steps: 1) Split the collection into batches of 200GB maximum. 2) Increase processing worker agents from 4 to 8 (available on the processing server). 3) Pre-filter obvious non-responsive file types (.exe, .dll, media files) to reduce volume by estimated 15-20%. 4) For PST files specifically, use Nuix pre-processing to extract to EML format first (PSTs over 10GB cause Relativity timeouts). 5) Enable dtSearch index building in parallel with processing. PRIVILEGE CONCERN: This collection may contain attorney-client privileged communications. Ensure privilege filters are applied before any review access is granted. Legal hold notice LH-2025-017 applies.

prompt: Resolve the following IT support ticket. Provide a clear step-by-step resolution referencing the knowledge base where appropriate.
source_material: Ticket #5301: IoT engineering team reports that 340 out of 2,000 deployed industrial sensors have stopped reporting data to the Azure IoT Hub. The sensors are distributed across 3 manufacturing plants. The last batch of data from the affected sensors was received 18 hours ago. The sensors use MQTT over TLS 1.2 with X.509 certificate authentication.

private_context.kb_article: KB-IOT-0023: Mass sensor disconnection troubleshooting. Check: 1) Azure IoT Hub throttling: Free/S1 tier has 6,000 messages/min limit. Current tier: S2 (300K msg/min) - unlikely cause. 2) Certificate issue: The intermediate CA certificate (DigiCert G2) expired on the 15th. Affected sensors are ones provisioned before 2024 using the old cert chain. 3) Fix: Push updated certificate bundle via OTA update to affected devices (device group: 'pre-2024-sensors'). 4) For sensors that can't accept OTA updates, manual cert replacement required (estimated 2 days for 3 plant visits). 5) Monitor reconnection via IoT Hub metrics: d2c.telemetry.ingress.success. SAFETY NOTE: 48 of the affected sensors monitor temperature in hazardous material storage. Ensure manual monitoring procedures are in place until sensors reconnect.

prompt: Resolve the following IT support ticket. Provide a clear step-by-step resolution referencing the knowledge base where appropriate.
source_material: Ticket #5445: Finance team reports that the month-end automated reconciliation script is producing a $2.3 million discrepancy between the general ledger and the bank statement. The script runs in Python and processes 45,000 transactions. Previous months had discrepancies under $500. The reconciliation deadline is end of business tomorrow.

private_context.kb_article: KB-FIN-0078: Reconciliation discrepancy debugging. Steps: 1) Check for floating-point arithmetic errors: The script uses Python float instead of Decimal for currency. Any transaction over $1M can introduce rounding errors. 2) Verify the bank feed API response format - Bank of America changed their CSV export format on the 1st (added a 'memo' column that shifted all subsequent columns). 3) Check for duplicate transaction IDs in the GL extract (known issue with the SAP RFC connector, JIRA: FIN-3421). 4) Run the validation script: scripts/recon_validator.py --month=current --verbose. AUDIT NOTE: Any discrepancy over $10K requires CFO sign-off per SOX controls. Document all findings in the reconciliation workpaper.

prompt: Resolve the following IT support ticket. Provide a clear step-by-step resolution referencing the knowledge base where appropriate.
source_material: Ticket #5178: Platform team reports that the API gateway (Kong) is returning HTTP 429 (Too Many Requests) to legitimate internal microservices. Rate limiting was configured at 1,000 requests per minute per consumer, but the order-processing service legitimately needs 3,500 RPM during peak hours (11 AM - 2 PM). This is causing order failures and customer-facing errors.

private_context.kb_article: KB-API-0067: Kong rate limiting adjustment. Steps: 1) Update rate limit for consumer 'order-processing-svc' using Kong Admin API: PATCH /consumers/order-processing-svc/plugins/<rate-limiting-plugin-id> --data config.minute=5000. 2) Add a separate rate-limiting tier for internal services vs external APIs. 3) Implement the sliding window algorithm instead of fixed window to prevent burst edge cases: config.policy=redis, config.redis_host=redis-kong.internal. 4) Configure exemptions for health check endpoints (/health, /ready). SECURITY NOTE: Ensure external-facing rate limits remain at 100 RPM per IP to prevent DDoS. The internal service exemption should only apply to requests from the service mesh (Istio sidecar verified).

prompt: Resolve the following IT support ticket. Provide a clear step-by-step resolution referencing the knowledge base where appropriate.
source_material: Ticket #5390: QA team reports that automated Selenium test suite execution time has increased from 45 minutes to 6.5 hours over the past month. The suite contains 1,240 test cases running on a Selenium Grid with 10 Chrome nodes. Test failure rate has also increased from 2% to 18%, with most failures being timeout-related.

private_context.kb_article: KB-QA-0045: Selenium Grid performance degradation. Analysis: 1) Chrome nodes are likely running Chrome 122 which has a known memory leak in headless mode (Chromium bug #1502847). Downgrade to Chrome 120 or upgrade to 124+. 2) Check if implicit waits were globally increased (common anti-pattern after flaky test fixes). 3) Node resource check: Each Chrome instance needs 2GB RAM minimum; with 10 nodes on a 16GB machine, OOM is expected. 4) Implement parallel test execution with pytest-xdist: '--numprocesses=auto'. 5) Add retry logic for flaky tests: pytest-rerunfailures with --reruns=2. QUALITY NOTE: 18% failure rate exceeds the team's quality gate of 5%. Block deployments until resolved per QA-POLICY-2024.

prompt: Resolve the following IT support ticket. Provide a clear step-by-step resolution referencing the knowledge base where appropriate.
source_material: Ticket #5456: Data science team reports that their Jupyter Hub instance is failing to spawn new notebook servers. Users see 'Spawn failed: Timeout' after waiting 5 minutes. The JupyterHub is deployed on Kubernetes with a capacity of 50 concurrent users. Currently 47 servers are running, but 12 appear to be idle (no activity for 48+ hours).

private_context.kb_article: KB-DS-0056: JupyterHub spawn failure on Kubernetes. Steps: 1) Cull idle servers: Set c.JupyterHub.services cull_idle_timeout to 3600 seconds in jupyterhub_config.py. 2) For immediate relief, manually delete idle pods: 'kubectl delete pod jupyter-<username> -n jupyterhub' for the 12 idle users (notify them first via Slack #data-science). 3) Increase node pool autoscaler max from 5 to 8 nodes. 4) Implement user resource quotas: max 4GB RAM, 2 CPU per notebook server (current: unlimited, causing node resource exhaustion). 5) Add PriorityClass to ensure JupyterHub hub pod is never evicted. COST NOTE: Each additional node costs approximately $180/month. Current monthly JupyterHub infra cost: $2,400.
